chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: Today's video is about AsyncIO  How we can get asynchronous tasks done  from python  and how it will save us a lot of time  Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: To understand AsyncIO  we have to understand that  AsyncIO is neither multi-threading nor multi-processing  It is just a way  to run your code concurrently

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: What is a typical execution flow in a python program?  Let's say you have a python program  Let's say I have a function 1  which I am running  and after this I have a function 2

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: which I am running  and function 3 which I am running  Now what will happen is  when function 1 will be completed  then function 2 will run and then function 3 will run

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: This is known by the world  So if I import you by time  and then import function 1  and then  time.sleep

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: Let's say I do 3 seconds  and then I print  func1  and then I make func2 and func3  So that time will be wasted

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: Because I know that you all know  that by doing this  first function 1 will execute  then function 2 will execute  then function 3 will execute

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: as you can see here  First of all, it will execute  func1 and then func2  and then  time.sleep

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: and then function 3  So this is a typical execution  flow which we all know  But sometimes it can happen  that you want to get some data

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: from the network  Let's say you want to download an image  Now while image 1 is downloading  Can't we download image 2  at the same time?

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: Technically, we can  But due to this  Python's shortcoming, you can't do this  So async programming  is used for this

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: It is used for such use cases  If I make any function  asynchronous function  So what will happen  It will become an async function

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: And now I can await it  Now as I run it  You can see it is giving me an error  It is saying that you have never awaited it  So what I can do

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: I can say  async, def, main  I can make a main function  And I can await all three of them  So they will run one by one

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: But what did it change? Nothing  In main, all three were running one by one  Now they will run one by one  So await means that you wait  for any async function to finish

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: Then run the next function  Then run the next function  Now how can I concurrently run  I can run it concurrently  So to run it, what can we do

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: We can say  asyncio.run  And then main as a function  So I think I didn't import asyncio  No problem

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: So now you can see  I have imported asyncio  asyncio  And if I run it  Then main function will run

chunks.text: Now you will say that nothing has changed  If we want to change things  How to change  We will change it by making a task  We will say asyncio.createTask

chunks.text: Now what does createTask do  CreateTask makes a task  Which will be executed  whenever  I will show you, as I wrote function1

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: Now I have made task  asyncio.createTask  And  This task will run  whenever time is available

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: Now what does it mean, I will show you  I will do one thing here  I have put time.sleep of 3  I will await it  and put asyncio.sleep

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: And put only 1 second sleep  So I will put 1 second sleep here  And here  I will put 4 seconds sleep  So now if I show you

chunks.text: After running it  So our function1 is scheduled  Function2 is running  When function2 was running  I got time to run function1

chunks.title: AsyncIO in Python
chunks.text: So by doing this  Whenever it will get time, it will run  But here we don't get a good organization  of things  That's why we use gather syntax

text: Today's video is about AsyncIO How we can get asynchronous tasks done from python and how it will save us a lot of time Let's go to the computer screen and let's get started To understand AsyncIO we have to understand that AsyncIO is neither multi-threading nor multi-processing It is just a way to run your code concurrently What is a typical execution flow in a python program? Let's say you have a python program Let's say I have a function 1 which I am running and after this I have a function 2 which I am running and function 3 which I am running Now what will happen is when function 1 will be completed then function 2 will run and then function 3 will run This is known by the world So if I import you by time and then import function 1 and then time.sleep Let's say I do 3 seconds and then I print func1 and then I make func2 and func3 So that time will be wasted Because I know that you all know that by doing this first function 1 will execute then function 2 will execute then function 3 will execute as you can see here First of all, it will execute func1 and then func2 and then time.sleep and then function 3 So this is a typical execution flow which we all know But sometimes it can happen that you want to get some data from the network Let's say you want to download an image Now while image 1 is downloading Can't we download image 2 at the same time? Technically, we can But due to this Python's shortcoming, you can't do this So async programming is used for this It is used for such use cases If I make any function asynchronous function So what will happen It will become an async function And now I can await it Now as I run it You can see it is giving me an error It is saying that you have never awaited it So what I can do I can say async, def, main I can make a main function And I can await all three of them So they will run one by one But what did it change? Nothing In main, all three were running one by one Now they will run one by one So await means that you wait for any async function to finish Then run the next function Then run the next function Now
